title_en: Hisn al-Muslim: The Fortress of the Muslim
content_id:
Hisn al-Muslim min Adhkar al-Kitab was-Sunnah — Benteng Muslim dari Dzikir-dzikir Kitab dan Sunnah — adalah kompilasi doa-doa dan dzikir-dzikir Islam kontemporer yang paling banyak didistribusikan di dunia. Pengarangnya, Said ibn Ali ibn Wahf al-Qahtani, adalah seorang ulama Saudi yang mengompilasi kumpulan doa-doa kenabian yang sahih berukuran saku ini sebagai referensi harian yang praktis bagi kaum Muslimin yang ingin mempertahankan kehidupan yang terstruktur oleh dzikir Qur'ani dan kenabian. Sejak pertama kali diterbitkan pada tahun 1980-an, buku ini telah dicetak dalam ratusan juta eksemplar dan diterjemahkan ke dalam lusinan bahasa.

Proyek Al-Qahtani secara eksplisit bersifat praktis: ia ingin menghasilkan kumpulan adhkar yang ringkas, mudah dibawa, dan dapat diandalkan keasliannya untuk penggunaan sehari-hari, dengan mengacu pada karya-karya klasik dasar dalam genre ini — terutama Al-Adhkar karya an-Nawawi, Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah karya Ibn as-Sunni, Al-Adab al-Mufrad karya al-Bukhari, dan enam koleksi hadis kanonis — sambil menerapkan standar kritik hadis kontemporer untuk mengecualikan tradisi-tradisi yang lemah dan palsu. Penyaringan kritis ini, yang didasarkan sebagian besar pada kriteria penilaian al-Albani dan ulama hadis kontemporer lainnya, membedakan Hisn al-Muslim dari kompilasi-kompilasi sebelumnya yang mencakup lebih banyak ragam tradisi.

Judul Hisn — yang berarti benteng atau puri — mencerminkan pemahaman Islam bahwa dzikir yang sahih kepada Allah memberikan perlindungan spiritual bagi mukmin dari bahaya, godaan, dan tipu daya setan. Al-Qur'an dan tradisi kenabian penuh dengan pernyataan bahwa dzikir kepada Allah yang teratur dan tulus menciptakan ruang spiritual yang terlindungi di sekeliling mukmin, dan judul Hisn al-Muslim menangkap fungsi perlindungan teologis dari adhkar yang dikandungnya.

Popularitas luar biasa buku ini telah menjadikannya salah satu ciri khas praktik ibadah Muslim Sunni kontemporer secara global, didistribusikan di masjid, pusat-pusat Islam, dan sebagai hadiah pada pernikahan dan kesempatan lainnya. Ukurannya yang ringkas — kebanyakan edisi muat di saku kemeja — telah berkontribusi pada kepraktisannya dan penyebarannya secara luas.
content_en:
Hisn al-Muslim min Adhkar al-Kitab was-Sunnah — Fortress of the Muslim from the Remembrances of the Book and the Sunnah — is the most widely distributed contemporary compilation of Islamic supplications and remembrances in the world. Its author, Said ibn Ali ibn Wahf al-Qahtani, is a Saudi scholar who compiled this pocket-sized collection of authenticated prophetic supplications as a practical daily reference for Muslims seeking to maintain a life structured by Quranic and prophetic remembrance. Since its first publication in the 1980s, it has been printed in hundreds of millions of copies and translated into dozens of languages.

Al-Qahtani's project was explicitly practical: he wanted to produce a concise, portable, and reliably authenticated collection of adhkar for everyday use, drawing on the foundational classical works in the genre — particularly Al-Nawawi's Al-Adhkar, Ibn as-Sunni's Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah, al-Bukhari's Al-Adab al-Mufrad, and the canonical six hadith collections — while applying contemporary hadith critical standards to exclude weak and fabricated traditions. This critical filtering, based largely on the assessment criteria of al-Albani and other contemporary hadith scholars, distinguishes Hisn al-Muslim from earlier compilations that included a wider range of traditions.

The title Hisn — meaning fortress or citadel — reflects the Islamic understanding that authentic remembrance of God provides the believer with spiritual protection from harm, temptation, and the devices of Shaytan. The Quran and the prophetic tradition are full of declarations that regular, sincere remembrance of God creates a protected spiritual space around the believer, and Hisn al-Muslim's title captures this protective theological function of the adhkar it contains.

The book's extraordinary popularity has made it one of the defining features of contemporary Sunni Muslim devotional practice globally, distributed at mosques, Islamic centers, and as gifts at weddings and other occasions. Its conciseness — most editions fit in a shirt pocket — has contributed to its practical utility and widespread adoption.
content_ar:
Hisn al-Muslim min Adhkar al-Kitab was-السنة النبوية — Fortress of the Muslim from the Remembrances of the Book and the السنة النبوية — is the most widely distributed contemporary compilation of Islamic supplications and remembrances in the world. Its author, Said ibn Ali ibn Wahf al-Qahtani, is a Saudi scholar who compiled this pocket-sized collection of authenticated prophetic supplications as a practical daily reference for Muslims seeking to maintain a life structured by Quranic and prophetic remembrance. Since its first publication in the 1980s, it has been printed in hundreds of millions of copies and translated into dozens of languages.

Al-Qahtani's project was explicitly practical: he wanted to produce a concise, portable, and reliably authenticated collection of adhkar for everyday use, drawing on the foundational classical works in the genre — particularly Al-Nawawi's Al-Adhkar, Ibn as-Sunni's Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah, al-Bukhari's Al-Adab al-Mufrad, and the canonical six الحديث collections — while applying contemporary الحديث critical standards to exclude weak and fabricated traditions. This critical filtering, based largely on the assessment criteria of al-Albani and other contemporary الحديث scholars, distinguishes Hisn al-Muslim from earlier compilations that included a wider range of traditions.

The title Hisn — meaning fortress or citadel — reflects the Islamic understanding that authentic remembrance of God provides the believer with spiritual protection from harm, temptation, and the devices of Shaytan. The القرآن الكريم and the prophetic tradition are full of declarations that regular, sincere remembrance of God creates a protected spiritual space around the believer, and Hisn al-Muslim's title captures this protective theological function of the adhkar it contains.

The book's extraordinary popularity has made it one of the defining features of contemporary Sunni Muslim devotional practice globally, distributed at mosques, Islamic centers, and as gifts at weddings and other occasions. Its conciseness — most editions fit in a shirt pocket — has contributed to its practical utility and widespread adoption.
